decisions.title: Explore V1 Sunset Timeline: March 31 Hard Cutoff
decisions.context: Multiple signals indicate Explore V1 sunset in progress (ENG-9551, ENG-9549, ENG-9276). Analytics must be renamed by March 31 GA deadline. Decision needed: enforce hard cutoff or allow grace period for stragglers.

arguments.for: Hard cutoff drives urgency, reduces maintenance burden, aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar by consolidating user experience in V2
arguments.against: Grace period reduces support burden from confused users, allows time for final migration edge cases, maintains customer goodwill

decisions.title: Channel List V2 Scope: Ship Pinned Elements Before Full Redesign
decisions.context: Three related tickets (ENG-9115, ENG-9077, ENG-9116) suggest Channel List V2 is complex. CPU issues (ENG-8960) indicate performance is critical. Decision: ship top-level pinned elements independently as quick win, or wait for full V2?

arguments.for: Incremental delivery reduces risk, gets pinned elements to power users faster, validates performance improvements independently
arguments.against: Two releases increase coordination overhead, may confuse users with partial redesign, could create tech debt if V2 architecture differs

decisions.title: Ingest Service Memory Growth: Immediate Hotfix or Wait for Root Cause
decisions.context: Two critical tickets (ENG-9429, ENG-9391) indicate unbounded memory growth in ingest service. This threatens 'Build the Moat' pillar (reliability at scale). Decision: deploy immediate circuit breaker vs. wait for root cause fix.
decisions.blockedBy: Need memory profiling data to determine if hotfix is safe without causing data loss

arguments.for: Hotfix prevents potential outages, buys time for proper root cause analysis, demonstrates responsiveness to reliability issues
arguments.against: Circuit breaker could drop data or create inconsistency, may mask root cause making it harder to diagnose, creates technical debt

decisions.title: AWS SSO Keycloak Decoupling: Prioritize vs. Other Infra Work
decisions.context: ENG-9547 identifies circular dependency risk in auth infrastructure. This is foundational but not customer-facing. Decision: prioritize this technical debt or focus engineering on customer-visible features.

arguments.for: Circular dependencies are existential risk, aligns with 'Build the Moat' (operational resilience), prevents future outages during auth failures
arguments.against: No customer value, diverts 2+ weeks of senior eng time from Explore V2 and Canvas, current system is stable enough

decisions.title: Compaction Strategy Rollout: Phased vs. Big Bang
decisions.context: ENG-9414 introduces adaptive merge strategy (skip-merge, spilling, bulk pre-stage) for compaction. Major query engine change. Decision: roll out to 10% of customers first or deploy to all.

arguments.for: Phased rollout reduces blast radius, allows performance comparison across cohorts, aligns with 'Nail the Core' (query reliability)
arguments.against: Phased rollout creates operational complexity (two code paths), delays time-to-value for majority of customers, requires more monitoring

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy decision load this week due to Explore V1 sunset deadline (Mar 31) and ingest service memory issues requiring immediate attention. Consider async decision-making for AWS SSO decoupling to preserve focus time.
alerts: March 31 deadline in 8 days: 4 open decisions need resolution this week
Ingest service memory issue escalating: needs decision within 24h to prevent outage risk
Engineering capacity at 95%: new priorities will require de-scoping or timeline extension
